name: Mechanism deriving fermion chirality from structural inputs
closability_note: The puzzle is partly definitional: chiral fermions ARE required for the SM's anomaly-cancelled gauge structure, so any UV completion that gives SU(3)xSU(2)xU(1) with the right matter representations explains chirality at that level. But the deeper question — why are the fermions chiral at all, why not a vector-like spectrum at some high scale that breaks to a chiral IR? — remains open. String compactifications produce chiral spectra from intersection numbers or Calabi-Yau topology, but the choice of compactification is itself unconstrained. A closing mechanism would have to derive chirality from a topological invariant or a symmetry-protection argument, not just observe that 'this compactification happens to be chiral'.
summary: The SM is chiral (left- and right-handed fermions transform differently under SU(2)). No framework in the catalog derives this from structural inputs — most assume it, GUTs unify within a chirality but don't constrain the choice, and string compactifications produce chiral spectra without deriving why.
what_closes_it: A mechanism that forces a chiral spectrum from a structural input — topological (an index theorem on the compactification manifold that rules out vector-like spectra), symmetry-based (a discrete symmetry whose anomaly cancellation requires chirality), or dynamical (a phase transition in the early universe that selects the chiral phase from a non-chiral parent). The F-theory route is currently strongest: intersection numbers of matter curves on the GUT divisor determine the chiral index, and consistency conditions (D7-brane tadpole cancellation, flux quantisation) impose constraints. But no published F-theory model derives the SM chiral content without input choices.
scale_hint: GUT scale or higher (10^16 - 10^19 GeV). Chirality is set at compactification or by a topological constraint at the UV; low-energy mechanisms cannot generate it from a vector-like parent without breaking gauge invariance.

principles_at_stake.note: Gauge anomaly cancellation in the SM requires the specific chiral content (Y_L + Y_R = 0 across each family). The gap-closer must respect anomaly cancellation while deriving the chirality assignment.

literature_anchors.citation: Candelas, Horowitz, Strominger, Witten, 'Vacuum configurations for superstrings', Nucl. Phys. B 258, 46 (1985)
Beasley, Heckman, Vafa, 'GUTs and Exceptional Branes in F-theory - I', JHEP 01 (2009) 058
Donagi and Wijnholt, 'Breaking GUT Groups in F-Theory', Adv. Theor. Math. Phys. 15, 1523 (2011)
